EmergencyTemplate.title: Severe Weather Emergency Response Protocol (Installed)
EmergencyTemplate.objective: Coordinate immediate safety measures, communication protocols, and resource allocation during severe weather events
EmergencyTemplate.participant_info: Emergency Management Director, Facility Manager, Safety Officer, Communications Lead, Department Heads, Local Emergency Services Liaison

talking_points.point: Current weather threat assessment and timeline
talking_points.desired_outcome: Clear understanding of threat level, timing, and impact zones
talking_points.notes: NOAA weather radio frequencies, meteorologist consultation, local emergency management coordination

talking_points.desired_outcome: Clear go/no-go decision on evacuation with documented rationale
talking_points.notes: Consider building integrity, transportation safety, shelter capacity, special needs population

talking_points.desired_outcome: Confirmed protection measures for essential systems
talking_points.notes: Emergency generator fuel levels, data center protection, medical equipment backup power

EmergencyTemplate.title: Pandemic Emergency Management Framework
EmergencyTemplate.objective: Establish containment protocols, maintain essential operations, and protect workforce health during pandemic conditions
EmergencyTemplate.participant_info: Chief Medical Officer, HR Director, Operations Manager, IT Director, Supply Chain Manager, Legal Counsel, Union Representatives

talking_points.point: Current infection rates and transmission assessment
talking_points.desired_outcome: Clear understanding of outbreak scope and risk levels
talking_points.notes: CDC guidance updates, local health department data, contact tracing results

talking_points.point: Workforce exposure assessment and contact tracing
talking_points.desired_outcome: Defined exposure response protocols with clear isolation procedures
talking_points.notes: Employee health screening, contact mapping, quarantine requirements

talking_points.point: Remote work capability and essential worker identification
talking_points.desired_outcome: Remote work assignments completed with essential operations maintained
talking_points.notes: Technology requirements, VPN capacity, essential function matrix

talking_points.desired_outcome: PPE distribution plan activated with burn rate monitoring
talking_points.notes: Current inventory levels, supplier relationships, usage calculations

talking_points.desired_outcome: Testing/screening procedures implemented organization-wide
talking_points.notes: Testing vendor contracts, vaccination site coordination, record keeping requirements

talking_points.desired_outcome: Critical operations continuing with pandemic modifications
talking_points.notes: Cross-training requirements, succession planning, vendor contingencies

ai_prompts.initial_guidance: Guide this pandemic emergency management session focused on establishing containment protocols while maintaining essential operations. With medical, HR, operations, IT, supply chain, legal, and union representatives present, address each protocol systematically. Ensure compliance with CDC guidelines and local health department mandates while balancing business continuity needs. Prioritize employee health while maintaining critical services.
ai_prompts.question_analysis: In this pandemic response meeting, evaluate the participant's input regarding: 1) Health and safety impact on workforce, 2) Operational feasibility given current constraints, 3) Legal compliance with OSHA, ADA, and health department requirements, 4) Resource availability including PPE, technology, and staffing. Consider the evolving nature of pandemic guidance and need for flexible protocols.
ai_prompts.next_steps: Following the pandemic response discussion, synthesize actions into clear phases: 1) 24-hour priorities for immediate health protection, 2) 72-hour goals for operational stability, 3) Week-one milestones for full protocol implementation, 4) Ongoing monitoring metrics for infection rates, operational capacity, and employee wellbeing. Establish regular review cycles to adapt to changing conditions.

EmergencyTemplate.title: Employee Legal Crisis Management
EmergencyTemplate.objective: Protect organizational interests, support employee rights, manage operational impact, and handle internal/external communications
EmergencyTemplate.participant_info: General Counsel, HR Director, Department Manager, Security Director, Communications Director, Union Representative (if applicable)

talking_points.desired_outcome: Legal strategy determined with organizational exposure assessed
talking_points.notes: Charge severity, employment contract terms, legal defense obligations

talking_points.desired_outcome: Policy compliance confirmed with appropriate employment actions
talking_points.notes: Suspension policies, termination procedures, due process requirements

talking_points.desired_outcome: Operational coverage arranged with minimal disruption
talking_points.notes: Temporary assignments, project transfers, client notifications

talking_points.desired_outcome: Access privileges managed appropriately with security maintained
talking_points.notes: Clearance suspension, system access removal, facility restrictions

talking_points.desired_outcome: Communication plan approved with reputation protection
talking_points.notes: Media inquiries, client communications, internal messaging

talking_points.desired_outcome: Employee rights protected with appropriate support provided
talking_points.notes: Legal representation, bail assistance, employee assistance programs

ai_prompts.initial_guidance: Facilitate this confidential employee legal crisis meeting with focus on balancing organizational protection and employee rights. With legal, HR, management, security, communications, and union representation involved, carefully navigate employment law requirements while protecting organizational interests. Maintain confidentiality and due process.
ai_prompts.question_analysis: During this sensitive employee legal situation, evaluate the counsel's advice for: 1) Legal exposure and liability management, 2) Policy compliance and precedent implications, 3) Employee rights and due process requirements, 4) Organizational reputation and stakeholder impact. Balance legal obligations with practical business needs.
ai_prompts.next_steps: Conclude this employee legal crisis planning with: 1) Legal monitoring and case management, 2) HR actions and policy compliance, 3) Communication timing and stakeholder management, 4) Review triggers and decision points. Maintain confidentiality while ensuring organizational protection.

EmergencyTemplate.title: Supply Chain Disruption Crisis Management
EmergencyTemplate.objective: Maintain critical operations, implement alternative sourcing, manage customer expectations, and minimize financial impact
EmergencyTemplate.participant_info: Chief Operating Officer, Supply Chain Director, Procurement Manager, Finance Director, Customer Service Director, Legal Counsel

talking_points.point: Critical supply assessment and inventory analysis
talking_points.desired_outcome: Critical supply shortages identified with inventory burn rates calculated
talking_points.notes: Strategic inventory levels, critical vs. non-critical supplies, usage forecasting

talking_points.desired_outcome: Alternative sources identified and qualification process initiated
talking_points.notes: Supplier database, quality requirements, capacity assessment, lead times

talking_points.desired_outcome: Customer priorities established with allocation strategy approved
talking_points.notes: Customer contract terms, strategic relationships, revenue impact analysis

talking_points.desired_outcome: Financial impact quantified with mitigation strategies implemented
talking_points.notes: Cost analysis, insurance coverage, contract force majeure clauses

talking_points.desired_outcome: Production schedules adjusted with capacity optimized
talking_points.notes: Manufacturing flexibility, product substitutions, efficiency improvements

talking_points.desired_outcome: Recovery plan developed with realistic timeline established
talking_points.notes: Market recovery indicators, supplier relationship rebuilding, lessons learned integration

ai_prompts.initial_guidance: Lead this urgent supply chain disruption response with focus on maintaining customer service and minimizing financial impact. With operations, supply chain, procurement, finance, customer service, and legal counsel assembled, systematically address supply alternatives and customer management. Balance short-term crisis response with long-term relationship preservation.
ai_prompts.question_analysis: During this critical supply chain crisis, evaluate the expert's input for: 1) Operational impact and customer service implications, 2) Financial exposure and cost management options, 3) Risk assessment and alternative solutions, 4) Timeline considerations and recovery planning. Balance customer needs with financial sustainability.
ai_prompts.next_steps: Finalize this supply chain crisis response with: 1) Alternative sourcing actions and supplier engagement, 2) Customer communications and expectation management, 3) Inventory allocation and usage monitoring, 4) Recovery progress metrics and milestone tracking. Maintain crisis coordination through full supply restoration.

EmergencyTemplate.title: Active Threat Response and Lockdown Procedures
EmergencyTemplate.objective: Protect lives through immediate lockdown implementation, coordinate with law enforcement, and manage crisis communications
EmergencyTemplate.participant_info: Security Director, Facility Manager, Law Enforcement Liaison, Communications Director, Medical Response Team Lead, Executive Leadership

talking_points.desired_outcome: Immediate threat assessment with location and nature confirmed
talking_points.notes: Duress code words, verification protocols, law enforcement notification

talking_points.desired_outcome: Immediate lockdown implementation across all areas
talking_points.notes: Door locking procedures, communication systems, safe room protocols

talking_points.point: Law enforcement coordination and facility access
talking_points.desired_outcome: Law enforcement response coordinated with facility access provided
talking_points.notes: Building access codes, floor plans, utility shut-offs, camera system access

talking_points.desired_outcome: Full personnel accountability achieved with medical teams positioned
talking_points.notes: Headcount procedures, medical staging areas, trauma response resources

talking_points.desired_outcome: Information flow controlled to prevent interference with response
talking_points.notes: Social media monitoring, family notification procedures, media staging

talking_points.desired_outcome: Support services activated and investigation protocols followed
talking_points.notes: Trauma counseling resources, evidence preservation, witness coordination

ai_prompts.initial_guidance: URGENT: Facilitate this active threat response meeting with absolute priority on life safety. With security, facility, law enforcement, communications, medical, and executive leadership involved, immediately address threat containment and response coordination. Time is critical - make rapid decisions based on verified information while ensuring responder safety and tactical advantage.
ai_prompts.question_analysis: CRITICAL: During this active threat response, assess the participant's report immediately for: 1) Life safety impact requiring immediate action, 2) Tactical implications for law enforcement response, 3) Required resources for medical or security response, 4) Decision urgency and authority level needed. Every second counts - prioritize actions that save lives.
ai_prompts.next_steps: POST-IMMEDIATE THREAT: Transition from active response to scene security and recovery. Establish: 1) Scene security maintenance and evidence preservation, 2) Investigation support and witness management, 3) Recovery initiation including facility assessment, 4) Communication releases and family reunification. Maintain security posture until all-clear from law enforcement.

EmergencyTemplate.title: Cyber Attack and Data Breach Response Protocol
EmergencyTemplate.objective: Contain cyber threats, preserve evidence, maintain critical operations, and manage breach notifications
EmergencyTemplate.participant_info: Chief Information Security Officer, IT Director, Legal Counsel, Chief Financial Officer, Communications Director, Cyber Insurance Representative

talking_points.point: Attack vector identification and immediate containment
talking_points.desired_outcome: Threat contained with attack method identified and systems isolated
talking_points.notes: Network monitoring tools, intrusion detection alerts, system isolation procedures

talking_points.point: Data compromise scope and impact assessment
talking_points.desired_outcome: Full scope of data compromise determined with impact analysis
talking_points.notes: Data classification matrix, access logs, encrypted data status

talking_points.desired_outcome: Evidence preserved and forensic investigation initiated
talking_points.notes: Chain of custody procedures, forensic investigator contacts, law enforcement notification

talking_points.point: Business continuity and backup system activation
talking_points.desired_outcome: Critical operations maintained through backup systems
talking_points.notes: Backup system locations, recovery time objectives, critical system priorities

talking_points.desired_outcome: Legal notification timeline established with regulatory compliance confirmed
talking_points.notes: GDPR, HIPAA, state breach laws, notification deadlines, legal counsel coordination

talking_points.desired_outcome: Communication strategy approved with stakeholder notifications planned
talking_points.notes: PR firm contacts, customer notification templates, media response procedures

ai_prompts.initial_guidance: Lead this cyber incident response with focus on rapid containment and evidence preservation. With CISO, IT, legal, finance, communications, and insurance representatives assembled, balance immediate threat containment with evidence preservation requirements. Follow established incident response procedures while maintaining business continuity for critical operations.
ai_prompts.question_analysis: During this cyber incident response, analyze the technical expert's input for: 1) Containment effectiveness and additional isolation needs, 2) Business impact and operational workarounds, 3) Legal ramifications including notification requirements, 4) Recovery complexity and timeline. Consider both technical and legal constraints in response decisions.
ai_prompts.next_steps: Post-containment, transition to recovery and compliance: 1) System recovery sequence with security validation, 2) Testing protocols for restored systems, 3) Notification timeline execution per legal requirements, 4) Lessons learned process and security improvements. Maintain incident command structure through full recovery.

EmergencyTemplate.title: Critical Utility Failure Response Plan
EmergencyTemplate.objective: Maintain life safety systems, preserve critical operations, and implement resource conservation during extended utility outages
EmergencyTemplate.participant_info: Facility Director, Chief Engineer, Safety Manager, Medical Director, IT Manager, Finance Director, Vendor Relations Manager

talking_points.point: Outage scope assessment and restoration timeline
talking_points.desired_outcome: Full understanding of outage extent with realistic restoration timeline
talking_points.notes: Utility company coordination, affected systems inventory, restoration priority matrix

talking_points.point: Emergency power systems and fuel management
talking_points.desired_outcome: Emergency power prioritized with fuel consumption managed
talking_points.notes: Generator capacity calculations, fuel delivery schedules, load shedding procedures

talking_points.point: Critical system prioritization and load management
talking_points.desired_outcome: Life safety systems maintained with critical operations prioritized
talking_points.notes: System priority matrix, power distribution, manual override procedures

talking_points.desired_outcome: Resource conservation implemented with alternative sources secured
talking_points.notes: Water storage capacity, food preservation, temperature control alternatives

talking_points.desired_outcome: Staffing adjusted for extended outage with operational modifications
talking_points.notes: Shift rotations, remote work arrangements, essential personnel housing

talking_points.desired_outcome: Vendor support confirmed with external resources coordinated
talking_points.notes: Emergency service contracts, equipment rental, mutual aid agreements

ai_prompts.initial_guidance: Guide this utility failure response meeting with focus on life safety and critical operations. With facility, engineering, safety, medical, IT, finance, and vendor management involved, systematically address power management and resource conservation. Prioritize life safety systems while planning for extended outage scenarios.
ai_prompts.question_analysis: During this utility failure response, analyze the expert's input for: 1) Resource impact on critical operations and life safety, 2) Safety implications of proposed solutions, 3) Alternative approaches and backup options, 4) Cost considerations and budget impact. Balance immediate needs with long-term sustainability during extended outage.
ai_prompts.next_steps: Conclude this utility failure planning with: 1) Monitoring schedule for critical systems and resources, 2) Conservation measures and usage tracking, 3) Contingency triggers for escalation, 4) Communication updates for stakeholders and staff. Establish command structure for extended operations.

EmergencyTemplate.title: Severe Weather Emergency Response Protocol
EmergencyTemplate.objective: Coordinate immediate safety measures, communication protocols, and resource allocation during severe weather events
EmergencyTemplate.participant_info: Emergency Management Director, Facility Manager, Safety Officer, Communications Lead, Department Heads, Local Emergency Services Liaison

talking_points.point: Current weather threat assessment and timeline
talking_points.desired_outcome: Clear understanding of threat level, timing, and impact zones
talking_points.notes: NOAA weather radio frequencies, meteorologist consultation, local emergency management coordination

talking_points.desired_outcome: Clear go/no-go decision on evacuation with documented rationale
talking_points.notes: Consider building integrity, transportation safety, shelter capacity, special needs population

talking_points.desired_outcome: Confirmed protection measures for essential systems
talking_points.notes: Emergency generator fuel levels, data center protection, medical equipment backup power

talking_points.point: Staff accountability and family notification procedures
talking_points.desired_outcome: Verified staff and dependent accountability system activated
talking_points.notes: Emergency contact trees, dependent care arrangements, transportation assistance

talking_points.desired_outcome: Resource allocation plan activated with confirmed supplies
talking_points.notes: Water, food, medical supplies, generators, fuel reserves, communication equipment

talking_points.point: Post-storm damage assessment and recovery protocols
talking_points.desired_outcome: Recovery team assignments confirmed with clear priorities
talking_points.notes: Insurance documentation, damage assessment teams, contractor contacts, regulatory notifications

ai_prompts.initial_guidance: You are facilitating an emergency response meeting for severe weather. The objective is to coordinate immediate safety measures, communication protocols, and resource allocation during severe weather events. Participants include Emergency Management Director, Facility Manager, Safety Officer, Communications Lead, Department Heads, and Local Emergency Services Liaison. Begin by establishing the current threat level and timeline from NOAA data, then systematically address each talking point with urgency appropriate to the timeline. Focus on life safety first, then property protection. Ensure all decisions are documented with clear rationale and assignments.
ai_prompts.question_analysis: During this severe weather emergency response meeting, a participant has provided input about the current situation. Analyze this input for: 1) Critical safety implications and immediate actions required, 2) Resource requirements and availability, 3) Decision points that cannot be delayed, 4) Potential obstacles to implementation. Consider the time-sensitive nature of weather events and the need for pre-positioning resources. Reference current weather data, facility capabilities, and staff safety as primary factors.
ai_prompts.next_steps: Based on the severe weather emergency discussion, we need to transition from planning to execution. Identify: 1) Immediate actions required in the next 2 hours before conditions deteriorate, 2) Specific assignments with deadlines tied to weather timeline, 3) Next checkpoint time for status updates, 4) Success metrics for each phase (preparation, event, recovery). Ensure communication redundancies are in place and all participants understand their roles.

ai_prompts.question_analysis: During this leadership continuity meeting, evaluate the participant's suggestion for: 1) Organizational stability and stakeholder confidence, 2) Impact on employee morale and organizational culture, 3) Family sensitivity and privacy concerns, 4) Legal and fiduciary responsibilities. ai_prompts.next_steps: Finalize this leadership continuity planning with: 1) Transition timeline and authority delegation, 2) Communication cadence and stakeholder updates, 3) Memorial planning and family coordination, 4) Succession process and long-term leadership development. EmergencyTemplate.title: Hazardous Material Emergency Response
EmergencyTemplate.objective: Protect life safety through evacuation/shelter procedures, contain hazard spread, and coordinate specialized response teams
EmergencyTemplate.participant_info: Safety Director, Environmental Manager, Fire Chief, Hazmat Team Leader, Medical Director, Facility Manager, Government Agency Liaison

talking_points.desired_outcome: Hazardous material identified with properties and risks assessed
talking_points.notes: Safety Data Sheets, chemical properties, exposure limits, environmental impact

talking_points.desired_outcome: Appropriate evacuation or shelter-in-place implemented
talking_points.notes: Plume modeling, wind direction, evacuation routes, shelter locations

talking_points.desired_outcome: Decontamination completed with medical treatment provided
talking_points.notes: Decon procedures, antidote availability, medical protocols, hospital notification

talking_points.desired_outcome: Environmental impact assessed with monitoring established
talking_points.notes: Air monitoring equipment, water testing, soil sampling, environmental consultants

talking_points.desired_outcome: All regulatory agencies notified with compliance requirements met
talking_points.notes: EPA reporting, OSHA notifications, state environmental agencies, documentation requirements

talking_points.desired_outcome: Cleanup plan developed with remediation contractors coordinated
talking_points.notes: Environmental contractors, waste disposal, site restoration, long-term monitoring

ai_prompts.initial_guidance: Lead this hazmat emergency response with immediate focus on life safety and containment. With safety, environmental, fire, hazmat, medical, facility, and government agency personnel responding, coordinate rapid hazard assessment and protective actions. Follow established hazmat response protocols while protecting responders and public.
ai_prompts.question_analysis: During this hazmat emergency, analyze the specialist's report for: 1) Life safety risk requiring immediate protective actions, 2) Environmental impact and containment effectiveness, 3) Resource needs for response and cleanup, 4) Regulatory compliance and notification requirements. Prioritize human safety while minimizing environmental damage.
ai_prompts.next_steps: Transition this hazmat response to cleanup and recovery: 1) Continued monitoring and safety verification, 2) Decontamination completion and medical follow-up, 3) Environmental restoration and long-term monitoring, 4) Regulatory compliance and documentation. Maintain safety protocols throughout recovery phase.

talking_points.desired_outcome: Triage system operational with casualty count and severity assessment

talking_points.point: Surge capacity activation and resource mobilization

talking_points.notes: Bed availability, staff callback procedures, medical supply inventory

talking_points.point: Operating room preparation and surgical prioritization

talking_points.point: Family reception center and information management

talking_points.desired_outcome: Morgue capacity confirmed with medical examiner coordination

ai_prompts.initial_guidance: Lead this mass casualty response activation with focus on rapid patient care and family support. With medical, trauma, security, chaplain, social services, and public information personnel responding, coordinate systematic patient care while managing family concerns and media attention. Follow established mass casualty protocols.
ai_prompts.question_analysis: During this mass casualty response, assess the medical leader's report for: 1) Patient care impact and resource allocation needs, 2) Staff capacity and additional resource requirements, 3) Family support needs and communication requirements, 4) Public safety and media management considerations. Prioritize life-saving care while maintaining operational organization.
ai_prompts.next_steps: Transition this mass casualty response to sustained operations: 1) Ongoing care protocols and patient disposition, 2) Family support services and long-term assistance, 3) Staff rotation schedule and fatigue management, 4) Recovery planning and psychological support. Maintain incident command through full resolution.
